note: Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/BirthdayProblem.lean:45 -- theorem birthday_measure : ℙ ({f | f.Injective} : Set (Fin 23 → Fin 365)) < 1 / 2, Theorem 93 of the 100 Theorems List titled 'Birthday Problem'; file docstring frames it as the standard probabilistic birthday-paradox statement (probability of a shared birthday among 23 people is >1/2).
verify_note: decl confirmed at exact cited line; theorem statement literally is the >50% collision-probability claim for 23 people -- honest exact match.

note: Mathlib/SetTheory/Cardinal/Aleph.lean:205 -- def omega : Ordinal ↪o Ordinal, with docstring at line 198 'omega 1 = ω₁ is the first uncountable ordinal'; the scoped notation ω₁ (line 211, docstring literally '`ω₁` is the first uncountable ordinal.') unfolds to `Ordinal.omega 1`.
verify_note: overclaimed exact: Ordinal.omega has type Ordinal ↪o Ordinal, a whole indexed family (omega 0=ω, omega 1=ω₁, omega 2=ω₂, ...), not itself an ordinal; Q3401770 denotes one specific ordinal (ω₁). The decl's own docstring only calls the *applied* term omega 1 (i.e. the ω₁ notation, not a citable decl name) the first uncountable ordinal -- joining the bare family-generator function is a category/precision mismatch, at best a generalization.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Prod.lean:43 -- def boxProd (G : SimpleGraph α) (H : SimpleGraph β) : SimpleGraph (α × β), notation G □ H (line 49). 'Box product' is the standard graph-theory name for the Cartesian product of graphs (as opposed to tensor/lexicographic/strong products); file docstring: 'This file defines the box product of graphs'.
verify_note: decl + line numbers confirmed verbatim; Wikidata Q3406706 description 'operation in graph theory' matches enwiki 'Cartesian product of graphs', and □ is the standard notation for exactly that operation -- honest exact match.

note: Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Pointwise/Set/Basic.lean:296 -- protected def mul : Mul (Set α) := ⟨image2 (· * ·)⟩, carrying an explicit to_additive docstring generating Set.add: 'The pointwise addition of sets `s + t` is defined as `{x + y | x ∈ s, y ∈ t}`' -- this is exactly the additive-combinatorics sumset A+B.
verify_note: line 296 confirmed verbatim (protected def mul + to_additive generating Set.add, present in declaration-data.json cache); Wikidata Q3489869 = 'set of all possible sums of an element of set A and an element of set B', matching {x+y|x∈s,y∈t} exactly.

note: Mathlib/Algebra/Ring/Periodic.lean:45 -- def Periodic [Add α] (f : α → β) (c : α) : Prop := ∀ x, f (x + c) = f x. This is the general periodicity predicate for any function out of an additive type; a periodic sequence is the special case α = ℕ (or ℤ). Mathlib already tags this same decl @[wikidata Q184743] for the distinct, more general 'periodic function' QID -- Q3503302 (periodic sequence) is the discrete-domain special case of that same predicate, not a separately-defined decl, hence generalization rather than exact.
verify_note: decl at line 44-46 confirmed, already carries @[simp, wikidata Q184743] in source exactly as claimed; Wikidata P279 confirms Q3503302 is literally subclass_of Q184743 -- generalization label is honest, not overclaimed.

note: Path verified as key libraries.Mathlib.modules.ModelTheory in catalog/data/hierarchy.json (n_decls 1626). Mathlib/ModelTheory/Basic.lean:10-14 -- 'This file defines first-order languages and structures... FirstOrder.Language ... FirstOrder.Language.Structure' -- Mathlib's model theory library formalizes first-order (predicate) logic syntax/semantics (Language, Term, Formula, Structure, Realize); predicate logic is a field-level concept with no single defining decl, so a folder container is the honest join.
verify_note: hierarchy.json key + n_decls=1626 confirmed, but the folder's actual content (Substructures, ElementaryMaps, Ultraproducts, Fraisse, Skolem, Satisfiability, DirectLimit) is model theory proper, not predicate logic; a distinct QID Q467606 'model theory' already exists and is already live-brain-tagged to path:Mathlib/ModelTheory/Semantics with an exact decl bond -- assigning the broader parent folder to a different, narrower QID (predicate logic) misattributes the field-level home and duplicates/conflicts with the existing model-theory container.
